codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata, handoff, prior failing review, fix transcript skeleton, the original t16 commit diff, the fix commit diff, and the current `src/model/data_builder.py` / `tests/test_data_builder.py` code path. The same-day leakage failure is adequately addressed in scope: the reviewed fix changed training-row assembly so same-day history is no longer reused while rows still advertise a prior-day `as_of_timestamp`, and the current builder path preserves that contract by computing feature modules per game date with `< target_day` cutoffs before assembling rows. The doubleheader regression now asserts later same-day rows keep the same prior-day timestamp and retain identical pregame feature values rather than absorbing the earlier game result. I did not find a new blocking issue within this fix's scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Windows `typecheck_windows` command in the shared services manifest appears unreliable in this exec environment, so the worker had to replace it with a custom `py_compile` sweep. That gap is actionable shared state because the manifest is documented as the single source of truth for project commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml:9` defines `typecheck_windows` with PowerShell `$env:` / `ForEach-Object` syntax; the fix handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T00-50-08-012Z__fix-t16-sameday-leakage__5b3c2899-4880-4072-b0ef-cd9e5c12962f.json:51-61` records `followedProcedure: false` because that snippet was unreliable and the worker had to validate syntax another way.

summary: Pass. This fix resolves the prior blocking same-day/doubleheader leakage problem by keeping all same-date training rows on the documented prior-day cutoff and backing that behavior with a regression test. No new blocking issue was found in scope, though the shared Windows typecheck command remains a manifest gap.
